arts.ttl: Collymore’s column: Champions League chances for Aston Villa, PSG crying, Man United and Spurs in the Europa League, Newcastle woes for Carabao Cup final, and more
arts.sum: In his exclusive column for CaughtOffside, former Aston Villa and Liverpool forward Stan Collymore discusses some of football’s biggest talking points, including the prospect of a Liverpool-Aston Villa quarter-final clash in the Champions League, Manchester United’s possible “redemption” in the Europa League, the upcoming Carabao Cup final and much more. — Aston Villa were fortunate […] The post Collymore’s column: Champions League chances for Aston Villa, PSG crying, Man United and Spurs in the Europa League, Newcastle woes for Carabao Cup final, and more appeared first on CaughtOffside.

arts.ttl: Aston Villa star has everything he needs to debunk Arne Slot's Liverpool boast
arts.sum: The Liverpool head coach has been reacting to his team's Champions League victory over Paris St Germain and the performance of one man Emi Martinez’s status as the best goalkeeper on the planet has not been up for debate in the last few years, indeed the Aston Villa star has two consecutive Yashin Trophies to underline his credentials.

arts.ttl: “Very happy to have done it” – Player glad he moved to Aston Villa despite brief period there
arts.sum: Nicolo Zaniolo had a mixed loan stint at Aston Villa during the 2023/24 season. The Italian had joined on loan from Galatasaray. After his time in Birmingham, he joined Atalanta on loan and in January,he moved to Fiorentina. arts.ttl: Preston v Villa and Brighton-Forest FA Cup ties on BBC
arts.sum: The BBC will show the FA Cup quarter-final between Preston - the only club outside the top flight left in the competition - against Aston Villa.

arts.ttl: Man City 'hugely impressed' by £50m Aston Villa attacker as sensational Jack Grealish transfer swap assessed
arts.sum: Manchester City reportedly express an interest in signing Aston Villa attacker Jacob Ramsey, who could cost the Citizens in excess of £50m.

arts.ttl: Analysis: Asensio can show he is much more than a supersub at Villa
arts.sum: Marco Asensio has now scored five goals in seven appearances for Aston Villa in all competitions, including this week's penalty in the UEFA Champions League - a competition he won three times with Real Madrid. The campaign might not end in qualification for next season’s Champions League (the Opta supercomputer gives 10th-placed Villa just a 3.9 per cent chance of finishing in the Premier League’s top five), and winning the whole thing this season is even less likely.
